agent_notes: Harrow produced a compact PAINTS list rather than 30-35 picks: the borough has good local heritage/craft culture, but very few independent bookshop-plus-coffee venues and no Creative Walk route inside the borough on the PAINTS v6 approved safety list.

Several museum/gallery picks are demoted to Tier B because no same-category backup was found within the required 10-minute walking radius; host must confirm live Google ratings, group walk-in tolerance and any event-date closures before publishing.

Current exhibition evidence is strongest for Headstone Manor's Stories of Harrow and Heath Robinson Museum's 10th anniversary programme; Harrow Arts Centre is valuable locally but the gallery's public access is weekday-hours constrained.

venues.opening_hours: Bar: Sun-Thu 12:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-00:00; kitchen hours vary. Open on 2026-07-04 appears yes from standard Saturday hours; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm no private hire.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins appear accepted for drinks; booking offered/recommended for meals and groups. Group of 8-12: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable early evening or Sunday afternoon if garden/side tables are free; call ahead strongly advised.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic pub with multiple rooms and raised beer garden; group should avoid peak Saturday dinner without host check.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Grade II, heritage-pub interior gives Harrow-on-the-Hill a proper story-led drinks stop rather than a generic bar. The old-school rooms, real ales and hill setting make it useful for object-led Show & Tell conversation. It is one of the few Harrow drinks venues where the building itself carries the event.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub traces roots to the early 18th century; the current building dates from the early 1900s and is noted for CAMRA heritage pub interest.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cask ale, wine, pub food, garden seating; not a natural-wine venue.
venues.safety_notes: Harrow-on-the-Hill is well connected but hilly and quieter late at night; recommend daylight/early evening arrivals and walking back together to the station after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approximately 4 minutes walk via West Street/Middle Road; verify on Google Maps day-of.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not natural-wine led, and peak meal periods may split a group unless host has confirmed table availability.

venues.opening_hours: Mon-Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-22:30; food Mon-Tue 12:00-21:00, Wed-Sat 12:00-22:00, Sun 12:00-18:00. Open on 2026-07-04 appears yes from standard Saturday hours; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm no private hire.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins appear possible; booking/contact advised for groups and garden/pergola. Group of 8-12: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good candidate for 8-12 when garden/pergola is operating; do not rely on Saturday walk-in at peak.
venues.capacity_notes: Pub has garden/pizza-van setup and private pergola references; operationally better in clement weather.
venues.best_time_slot: Thu 17:00-19:00 or Sat 15:00-17:00 before dinner rush.
venues.why_fits_paints: A hilltop local pub close to Harrow School gives a visually legible, old-London-feeling setting. It is less historic-on-paper than The Castle but warmer operationally, with food and outdoor options. Best used as a relaxed Show & Tell pub rather than a wine-specialist event.
venues.anecdote_story: Its Middle Road location ties it to the Harrow-on-the-Hill school-and-village fabric; venue copy positions it as a local pub near Harrow School.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, seasonal food, pizza van weather permitting.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet residential hill streets; use early evening and direct route to Harrow-on-the-Hill station.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approximately 4 minutes walk via Middle Road/West Street; verify on Google Maps day-of.
venues.risks_watchouts: More food-pub than cultural drinks venue; Saturday private pergola/pizza operations must be checked.

venues.opening_hours: Museum and cafe Tue-Sun 10:00-16:00, closed Mon; free entry. Open on 2026-07-04 appears yes from standard Saturday hours; bank-holiday handling varies and must be confirmed.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum entry during usual opening hours; ticketing not required for ordinary visit. Group of 8-12: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm group entry and cafe seating.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a slow 90-minute visit plus cafe if group arrival is confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic site with multiple buildings and cafe; school/family programming can change atmosphere.
venues.best_time_slot: Sat or Sun 10:30-12:30; avoid family-event peaks.
venues.why_fits_paints: The strongest Harrow heritage pick: moated manor, local-history collection, and a current community-memory exhibition. It gives attendees a precise local story rather than a generic gallery wall. Demoted only because no same-category backup within 10 minutes was found.
venues.anecdote_story: Stories of Harrow foregrounds sixteen community organisations preserving migration, faith, displacement, entrepreneurship and everyday local memory in one of the UK's most diverse boroughs.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Permanent Harrow history displays plus Stories of Harrow in the Special Exhibitions Room, Mon 4 May-Sun 30 Aug 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime museum visit recommended; Headstone Manor Recreation Ground is not PAINTS-approved for Creative Walks, so do not reframe this as a park walk.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying museum/gallery backup found within 10 min - closest plausible cultural alternative is Harrow Arts Centre Gallery at roughly 35-45 min walk / local transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup failure keeps this Tier B; host must check current exhibition access and group cafe seating.

venues.venue_name: Duck Pond Market at Headstone Manor
venues.opening_hours: Event-specific. Duck Pond listed a Headstone Manor night market on Sun 24 May 2026 16:00-21:00 and other Headstone/May artisan dates; future Harrow dates after run date are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Market format is open-access by definition when running; no booking required for attendees unless specific event page says otherwise.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for 8-12 as a drifting market format if date/weather is confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor/heritage-barn setting; weather and event frequency are the operational constraints.
venues.best_time_slot: Market day 11:00-13:00 for daytime editions or first hour for night market editions.
venues.why_fits_paints: Duck Pond's artisan-food-and-craft format fits the maker-led Market Days brief, and Headstone Manor gives it a strong cultural anchor. The setting is more memorable than a high-street stall lineup. Tier B because the recurring future Harrow schedule and 15-minute market backup are not robust enough.
venues.anecdote_story: Duck Pond positions itself around independent crafters, food and ethical businesses; Headstone Manor supplies the Harrow-history backdrop.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Artisan food, craft, vintage/gift/homewares depending on trader roster.
venues.safety_notes: Use daytime/early evening market windows; do not route attendees into Headstone Manor Recreation Ground as a Creative Walk.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying alternative market/craft destination found within 15 min walk or simple transit inside Harrow.
venues.risks_watchouts: Must not be published until a live Harrow market date is confirmed; weather plan needed.

venues.opening_hours: Thu-Sun and bank holiday Mondays 11:00-16:00, except Christmas, Boxing Day and New Year's Day. Open on 2026-07-04 appears yes from standard Saturday hours; host must confirm special-event access.
venues.walk_in_policy: Tickets bookable online and available in the museum during normal opening hours; group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good small-museum group if arrival is staggered and cafe seating at Daisy's is checked.
venues.capacity_notes: Small galleries; ideal for quieter weekday/early weekend slots rather than busy family-fair windows.
venues.why_fits_paints: A purpose-built museum devoted to one very particular illustrator is exactly the kind of precise, story-rich cultural oddity PAINTS likes. The 2026 10th-anniversary context adds freshness. Demoted because Pinner has no same-category 10-minute backup.
venues.anecdote_story: William Heath Robinson lived in Pinner from 1908-1918; the museum celebrates his famously intricate comic contraptions and wider illustration work.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Permanent Heath Robinson displays plus Heath Robinson Unseen / 10th Anniversary Exhibition listed from 4 July 2026; Daisy's in the Park shares West House premises as independent cafe.
venues.safety_notes: Pinner Memorial Park is not on the PAINTS Creative Walk safety list, so this should be treated as a museum visit only, not a park walk.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying museum/gallery backup found within 10 min - closest plausible option is Headstone Manor & Museum by local transit, not walking radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small scale and ticketing/group walk-in uncertainty; do not use as Creative Walk despite park setting.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Eastcote Village / Eastcote or Northwood Hills
venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Thu 11:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 11:00-00:00; Sunday hours need host confirmation. Open on 2026-07-04 appears yes from standard Saturday hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Appears to take bookings and casual pub visits; group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible for early drinks if beer garden/tables are free; not safe to assume for a matched group.
venues.capacity_notes: Traditional country-pub layout with garden; popular weather windows may fill.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is one of Harrow/Pinner's better character pubs: a country-pub texture, local warmth and enough visual grain to support Show & Tell. The Food Made Good 2026 signal helps operational/cultural credibility. Tier B because the category fit is pub-led rather than wine/story-led and the backup is weak.
venues.anecdote_story: Venue copy frames it as a traditional Eastcote Village country pub; social profiles flag a 2026 Food Made Good three-star award.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft beer, ale, seasonal British pub menu; not natural-wine led.
venues.safety_notes: Eastcote Village is quieter after dark; recommend groups arrive/depart together or use taxis from station late.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min - The Woodman Eastcote is the closest plausible pub but rating/review floor and walk time need host verification.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not independent-natural-wine coded; group walk-in and exact Sunday/bank-holiday hours need confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Mon-Thu 12:00-23:00, Fri 12:00-00:00, Sat 11:00-00:00, Sun 11:00-23:00. Open on 2026-07-04 appears yes from standard Saturday hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings available and recommended for busy nights/events; casual pub walk-ins likely. Group of 8-12: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable off-peak; quiz/live music evenings may make conversation difficult.
venues.capacity_notes: Traditional Pinner pub with pizza and garden reputation; may be lively rather than quiet.
venues.why_fits_paints: A strong Pinner high-street local with enough warmth, garden appeal and drink breadth to function as a secondary Show & Tell pub. It has a 2025 Best Bar None Harrow signal and occasional creative programming. It is not Tier A because it is less distinctive and less art/wine-led than PAINTS ideally wants.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub highlights Pinner's local Wheelbarrow race tradition and won Best Bar None Harrow 2025 plus a pub-garden award.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Lagers, ales, wines, cocktails and stone-fired pizzas; occasional paint-party style events.
venues.safety_notes: Very close to Pinner station and active high street; still avoid late departures for solo attendees.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min - Heath Robinson/Daisy's nearby is cafe/museum, not drinks category.
venues.risks_watchouts: Live events/quiz nights could drown conversation; group seating must be checked.

venues.opening_hours: Gallery access is based on regular weekly box office opening times, weekdays 10:00-17:00; broader weekend/evening access depends on exhibitors and staffing. 2026-07-04 Saturday access is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free/open viewing during stated gallery hours when exhibitions are installed; group of 8-12 access UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable only if exhibition access and quiet gallery time are pre-confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Gallery/corridor exhibition format rather than full museum; good add-on to a Hatch End cultural day, weaker as a standalone.
venues.why_fits_paints: Useful as a local-art access point, especially around Harrow Open Studios and community exhibitions. The building has arts-centre history and multiple creative rooms. It remains Tier B because public gallery hours and current exhibition depth are less robust than Headstone or Heath Robinson.
venues.anecdote_story: The centre occupies former Royal Commercial Travellers School buildings and is Harrow's dedicated arts venue; Harrow Open Studios uses it as a local creative anchor.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Contemporary/community art exhibitions in the gallery, corridors and feature wall depending on programme.
venues.safety_notes: Hatch End daytime/early evening is appropriate; confirm return route to Hatch End station after events.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying museum/gallery backup found within 10 min - Heath Robinson and Headstone Manor require local transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Saturday access and current show quality must be checked before use.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk parks/routes safety list. Although it contains Heath Robinson Museum and Daisy's in the Park, it cannot be sourced as a Creative Walk or park meeting route.
Not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk parks/routes safety list. Headstone Manor can be used as a museum/market venue, but the surrounding green space should not be reframed as a Creative Walk.
Ruislip Woods / Ruislip Lido is PAINTS safety-listed, but it sits outside Harrow borough in Hillingdon, so it was not used for a Harrow district file.
Chain bookshop and located in the busy central retail context; does not meet the independent books-and-coffee PAINTS requirement.
Would have been an excellent books-and-coffee fit, but live/local sources show it closed permanently in 2021.
Independent cafe and good meeting point for Heath Robinson Museum, but not a bookshop/book-wall/zine venue; also secondary review signal is below PAINTS rating floor.
Strong PAINTS fit in principle, but the main 2026 open-studios run ended on 21 June 2026 before this 4 July run date. Individual future exhibitions can be rechecked separately.

Historic building/story is interesting, but current operation reads as modern Indian restaurant, lounge and sports/cocktail venue rather than independent wine bar, natural-wine pub or character gastro pub for PAINTS Show & Tell.
Friendly local pub with real-ale signal, but it reads primarily as a sports/local pub and lacks enough PAINTS aesthetic/story/category specificity to clear 65.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Greenhill Art Pod - Stories of Harrow Community Voices
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Current exhibition signal is relevant, but venue rating/review floor, exact access model and group walk-in robustness could not be verified enough for inclusion as a standalone PAINTS venue.
